misconceptions.display_name: Divergence of the absolute series implies divergence
misconceptions.description: Thinks sum |a_n| diverging forces the alternating series to diverge; it may still converge conditionally.
trigger_phrases: the absolute values diverge so the series diverges
sum of 1/n diverges so the alternating one does too
if it's not absolutely convergent it diverges

misconceptions.display_name: Applying the test without checking b_n -> 0
misconceptions.description: Applies the Alternating Series Test even when b_n does not tend to 0; the test requires lim b_n = 0.
trigger_phrases: no need to check the limit of the terms

misconceptions.description: Concludes convergence from lim b_n = 0 alone, skipping that b_n must be decreasing.
trigger_phrases: the terms go to zero so it converges
